title: Layered Undo with Restore, Reset, Revert, and References · Graph Lens: Gita Restore Source Staged Worktree
summary: An incident spans unstaged edits, staged policy, three local commits, one published merge, and a release tag; each layer requires a different reversible repair. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita restore source staged worktree increment in layer-by-layer undo decision and verification matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use restore source, staged, worktree, pathspec, patch, and conflict-stage controls while preserving unrelated changes.
Classify the affected worktree, index, ref, graph, published history, and deployed-state layers before selecting an undo operation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Restore Source Staged Worktree
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G93 is a theory in a disposable repository. only the local tip ref and index must move while the working content stays available. The learner must predict HEAD moves one parent, the index resets, and working-tree changes remain for reconstruction, run git reset --mixed HEAD^, explain the local commit disappeared from main while its paths remain unstaged and recoverable, test this changed condition: treat the same commit as published and choose revert instead of moving shared history, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain refs/recovery/pre-undo at the original tip and verify reflog plus tree identity.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Restore Source Staged Worktree contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use restore source, staged, worktree, pathspec, patch, and conflict-stage controls while preserving unrelated changes.

content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Gita Restore Source Staged Worktree while producing layer-by-layer undo decision and verification matrix.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G93 is a worked example in a disposable repository. only the local tip ref and index must move while the working content stays available. The learner must predict HEAD moves one parent, the index resets, and working-tree changes remain for reconstruction, run git reset --mixed HEAD^, explain the local commit disappeared from main while its paths remain unstaged and recoverable, test this changed condition: treat the same commit as published and choose revert instead of moving shared history, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain refs/recovery/pre-undo at the original tip and verify reflog plus tree identity. Competency probe: Use restore source, staged, worktree, pathspec, patch, and conflict-stage controls while preserving unrelated changes. A defensible solution applies Gita Restore Source Staged Worktree, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to layer-by-layer undo decision and verification matrix.

content.code:
# Worked focus: gita-restore-source-staged-worktree — Use restore source, staged, worktree, pathspec, patch, and conflict-stage controls while preserving unrelated changes.
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 2
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: only the local tip ref and index must move while the working content stays available
git reset --mixed HEAD^
# expected: HEAD moves one parent, the index resets, and working-tree changes remain for reconstruction
# observation: the local commit disappeared from main while its paths remain unstaged and recoverable
# changed-case: treat the same commit as published and choose revert instead of moving shared history
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: retain refs/recovery/pre-undo at the original tip and verify reflog plus tree identity
# transfer-limit: preview untracked and ignored data separately before any real hard reset or clean operation
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Gita Restore Source Staged Worktree evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-restore-source-staged-worktree-1dy90y" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Restore Source Staged Worktree into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-restore-source-staged-worktree-1dy90y
# scenario-key: trace-1dy90y probe-y09yd1 boundary-y90y1d owner-1dy90yx fault-1dy90yz
# before-ref: main=a11ce1d candidate=b00b10y
# hypothesis: only the local tip ref and index must move while the working content stays available
git reset --mixed HEAD^
# expected: HEAD moves one parent, the index resets, and working-tree changes remain for reconstruction
# observation: the local commit disappeared from main while its paths remain unstaged and recoverable
# changed-case: treat the same commit as published and choose revert instead of moving shared history
# after-ref: main=a11ce1d candidate=c0ffeey
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: retain refs/recovery/pre-undo at the original tip and verify reflog plus tree identity
# transfer-limit: preview untracked and ignored data separately before any real hard reset or clean operation

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use restore source, staged, worktree, pathspec, patch, and conflict-stage controls while preserving unrelated changes.
